notes: Skill improves pass rate by +47% (93% vs 47%) across all 3 evals
Assertion 'Used live sources to verify versions' passes 100% in both configurations — non-discriminating for version-focused tasks
Decision format and verification log assertions pass 100% with skill, 0% without — these are the primary discriminators
CVE/security checking passes 67% with skill (missed sync-pre-commit-deps on eval 1) vs 33% without — skill helps but has a gap
SHA pinning passes in both configurations on eval 2 — baseline also SHA-pinned when the prompt mentioned 'best practices for action pinning'
Skill uses ~56% more tokens on average (50k vs 32k) but time is comparable (-19s on average) due to more parallel tool use
Eval 2 (GHA CI planning) shows the largest skill delta: 100% vs 40% — the broad planning task is where the skill is most additive
The one with-skill failure (eval 1, security check) was on sync-pre-commit-deps — a low-profile utility repo that may need explicit mention in the skill
